content:
What you can actually do

8 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Firmbee.com on UnsplashNon-Medium Members — Read Here
Think about the last thing you searched yesterday.
Maybe a symptom you were worried about. A relationship problem you couldn’t talk to anyone about. A financial situation you were embarrassed by. A political question you weren’t sure how to feel about. Something you’d never say out loud in a room full of people.
You typed it into Google because it felt private. A blank box, just you and the internet, no one watching.
Here’s the thing: someone was watching. A lot of someones, a lot of systems. And what they do with what they saw is more extensive, more permanent, and more consequential than most people have ever been told.
This isn’t speculation. Almost everything in this piece comes from Google’s own policies, published research, regulatory findings, and court documents. The unsettling part isn’t that Google is doing something secret. It’s that they’re doing it openly, and most people just haven’t read the fine print.
1. It Builds a Profile on You That Goes Back Years
